location.origin: Seattle. Mree (Marie Hsiao) is a Taiwanese-American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist based in Seattle. Duwamish ancestral territory. Part of Seattle's indie music scene.
location.formative: Seattle. YouTube and Bandcamp as primary platforms. Cover videos built initial audience. The digital-first career pathway -- the internet IS the venue. Small venues, bedroom recordings, intimate performances.
location.career: Mree (Marie Hsiao): Taiwanese-American singer-songwriter. Delicate folk/pop with ethereal vocals, ukulele, and layered arrangements. YouTube cover videos transitioned to original music. Self-released through Bandcamp and digital platforms. E=2. Third Asian-American artist in the engine.

labels: Independent / Self-released (digital platforms -- Bandcamp, YouTube, Spotify; maintains creative control; bedroom pop production; no major label infrastructure)

members.name: Mree (Marie Hsiao)
members.note: Solo artist. Taiwanese-American. Multi-instrumentalist. Creates delicate folk/pop with ethereal vocals and layered arrangements. YouTube cover videos built early audience. Third Asian-American artist in the engine.

associated_musicians.name: Briana Marela
associated_musicians.note: Both create layered vocal textures from a single voice multiplied. Marela uses live looping technology. Mree uses studio layering. Both create dreamlike atmospheres. The layered loop (72) versus the ethereal layering (77). Live process versus studio craft.

associated_musicians.name: Chong the Nomad
associated_musicians.note: Filipino-American (44) and Taiwanese-American (77). Different genres (Electronic vs. Folk/Pop), different energies (E=4 vs. E=2), same underrepresented identity in PNW music. Thirty-three degrees apart.

associated_musicians.name: Kris Orlowski
associated_musicians.note: Both operate at intimate scale with acoustic instrumentation. Orlowski's earnestness is direct and unadorned. Mree's earnestness is veiled in ethereal layers. Same sincerity, different textures.

associated_musicians.name: Chris Pureka
associated_musicians.note: Consecutive E=2 folk-adjacent artists (76-77). Pureka's voice is unprocessed, androgynous. Mree's voice is layered, ethereal. Different textures of intimacy. Both quiet, both folk-rooted.

associated_musicians.name: Sam Beam
associated_musicians.note: Both create intimate, hushed acoustic music. Beam's folk is literary and warm, guitar and baritone. Mree's folk/pop is ethereal and layered, ukulele and soprano. Complementary registers.

theory_nodes.concept: The ethereal layering (seventy-eighth acoustic element -- delicate vocals layered over ukulele and soft instrumentation, creating a gauzy dreamlike texture; multiple vocal takes woven together, harmonies with oneself, whispered overdubs floating beneath the primary melody; studio-crafted intimacy; the result is intimacy multiplied rather than diminished)
examples: Mree (degree 77) -- delicate folk/pop with ethereal vocals layered over ukulele. Multiple vocal takes create a gauzy, woven texture. Studio-crafted, deliberate, intimate.
Contrast with the androgynous register (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- single unprocessed voice. The ethereal layering is a voice multiplied, embracing processing.
Contrast with the layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- real-time vocal multiplication. The ethereal layering is studio-crafted, not performative.
Contrast with the communal harmony (degree 53, Fleet Foxes) -- many voices unified. The ethereal layering is one voice creating the illusion of community.
Contrast with the murmur (degree 45, Damien Jurado) -- whispered male intimacy. The ethereal layering is whispered female intimacy, multiplied.
The ethereal layering opens a textural dimension: not just what the voice sounds like but how many of itself it becomes.
theory_nodes.description: The ethereal layering is the seventy-eighth acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. Delicate vocals layered over acoustic instrumentation to create gauzy, dreamlike texture. One voice multiplied through studio craft. Intimacy multiplied rather than diminished.

ancestors: The androgynous register (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- single voice. The ethereal layering is multiplied voice.
The layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- live looping. The ethereal layering is studio layering.
The manuscript page (degree 57, Laura Gibson) -- literary care. The ethereal layering is textural care.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-eight acoustic elements across seventy-eight degrees. The ethereal layering follows the androgynous register (76) -- from identity expression to textural multiplication. Track element 79 at degree 78.

theory_nodes.concept: The YouTube-to-artist pipeline (seventy-eighth artist-city pattern -- artists who build initial audience through YouTube cover videos and digital content, then transition to original music; the platform IS the venue, the algorithm IS the booker; digital-first career pathway distinct from venue-based or identity-based audience building)
examples: Mree (degree 77) -- YouTube cover videos (ukulele covers of popular songs) built initial audience. Covers were reinterpretations, not imitations. YouTube channel became platform for original music.
Contrast with the identity artist (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- audience through lived identity. The YouTube pipeline is audience through digital content.
Contrast with the side project (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- existing fame as platform. The YouTube pipeline builds from zero.
Contrast with the international student (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- geographic movement. The YouTube pipeline is fixed location, digital distribution.
The YouTube pipeline represents a generational shift: from physical venues to digital platforms.
theory_nodes.description: The YouTube-to-artist pipeline is the seventy-eighth artist-city pattern. Artists who build audience through cover videos and digital content, then transition to originals. The platform IS the venue. Mree is the engine's first YouTube-pipeline artist.

ancestors: The identity artist (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- identity-based audience. The YouTube pipeline is content-based audience.
The side project (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- fame-based platform. The YouTube pipeline builds from zero.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-eight artist-city patterns across seventy-eight degrees. The YouTube pipeline follows the identity artist -- from analog identity to digital platform. Track pattern 79 at degree 78.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the delicate suspension (seventy-seventh deployment; the tension between the fragility of the sound and the resilience of the artistic practice; music that sounds like it might dissolve at any moment but persists; each vocal layer fragile alone but woven together creating something stronger; the gaps in the weave where silence shows through)
examples: Mree (degree 77) -- the blue note plays in the tension between ethereal fragility and sustained artistic practice. The music sounds like it might dissolve but it persists.
The blue note also plays in the layering: each vocal layer is fragile alone, but woven together they create something stronger. The gaps between threads are the blue note.
Contrast with the coming-out (degree 76, Chris Pureka) -- gap between assumed and actual identity. The delicate suspension is gap between apparent fragility and actual resilience.
Contrast with the reverb trail (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- spatial dissolution. The delicate suspension is textural dissolution.
Contrast with the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- two instruments approaching pitch. The delicate suspension is many layers approaching transparency.
theory_nodes.description: The delicate suspension is the seventy-seventh blue note deployment. The tension between fragility of sound and resilience of practice. Music that sounds like it might dissolve but persists. The gaps in the weave where silence shows through.

ancestors: Blue note -- the coming-out (degree 76, Chris Pureka): identity gap. The delicate suspension is resilience gap.
Blue note -- the reverb trail (degree 74, Poor Moon): spatial dissolution. The delicate suspension is textural dissolution.
All seventy-six prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-76)
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-seven deployments across seventy-eight degrees. Track deployment 78 at degree 78.

theory_nodes.concept: Folk/Pop at Genre Stage 59 -- bedroom pop with acoustic foundations; ukulele, guitar, layered vocals; folk texture meets pop structure; verse-chorus forms with intimate scale; the genre hybrid that sounds handmade but is structured for memorability
examples: Mree (degree 77) -- Folk/Pop. Genre Stage 59. Bedroom pop quality.
Follows Folk at Stage 58 (Pureka, 76). Consecutive folk-adjacent genres (58-59).
The folk element is textural (ukulele, guitar, intimate vocals). The pop element is structural (verse-chorus, hooks).
Contrast with Pureka (degree 76) -- confessional folk, unprocessed voice. Mree's folk/pop adds pop structure and layered production.
theory_nodes.description: Folk/Pop at degree 77 establishes Genre Stage 59. Bedroom pop with acoustic foundations. Ukulele-driven intimacy with pop melodic sensibility. Consecutive folk-adjacent genre (following Stage 58 Folk at degree 76).

ancestors: Folk at degree 76 (Chris Pureka) -- confessional folk. Mree's folk/pop adds pop structure.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 59 at degree 77. Folk/Pop. Consecutive folk-adjacent (58-59). Track genre at degree 78.

E=2 at prior deployments. 28th E=2. Dominant.

cross_references.relationship: Ethereal/layered. Both create layered vocal textures from a single voice multiplied. Marela uses live looping technology; Mree uses studio layering. Both arrive at dreamlike atmospheres. The layered loop (72) versus the ethereal layering (77). Live process versus studio craft.

Asian-American connection. Filipino-American (44) and Taiwanese-American (77). Different genres (Electronic vs. Folk/Pop), different energies (E=4 vs. E=2), same underrepresented identity. Thirty-three degrees apart.
Earnest folk. Both operate at intimate scale with acoustic instrumentation. Orlowski's earnestness is direct. Mree's is veiled in ethereal layers. Same sincerity, different textures.
Quiet folk, E=2. Consecutive E=2 folk-adjacent artists (76-77). Pureka's voice is unprocessed, androgynous. Mree's voice is layered, ethereal. Different textures of intimacy.
Hushed folk. Both create intimate, hushed acoustic music. Beam's folk is literary and warm, guitar and baritone. Mree's folk/pop is ethereal and layered, ukulele and soprano. Complementary registers.
